title: echium strictum subsp. gomerae
intro: Echium strictum subsp. gomerae, also known as Gomeran Viper's Bugloss, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) endemic to the island of La Gomera in the Canary Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Echium strictum subsp. gomerae include reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal properties.
